name: Mobilization

concept.conceptName: Promotion of walking using mobility aid
Assistance with getting out of bed

en.name: Mobilization
en.shortDescription: Patient mobilization — getting the patient moving (in/out of bed, standing, walking, with or without assistance); the act, not joint/organ mobilization.
en.longDescription:
## Definition & Clinical Context

Mobilization here means **patient mobilization** — the physiotherapy/nursing activity of getting a critically ill patient moving: assisting with movement in bed, sitting up, getting out of bed, standing, and walking. Early mobilization is a core ICU rehabilitation intervention that counters the deconditioning of prolonged bed rest. In source data this is a **procedure** record indicating that the patient was mobilized; there is no unit. It is recorded in intensive care and rehabilitation. It is the counterpart of the *Order not to mobilize* concept set.

## Included Concepts

The set lists the SNOMED procedures for patient mobilization and ambulation (curated — SNOMED has no single anchor for patient mobilization, and the broad 'mobilization' hierarchy mixes in joint and surgical mobilizations that are out of scope).

- *46272565 / 710950005 — Progressive mobility (SNOMED)*: progressive (staged) patient mobilization — the core ICU early-mobility concept.
- *46271859 / 710122006 — Promotion of physical mobility (SNOMED)*.
- *46272439 / 710803000 — Assistance with mobility (SNOMED)* and *37016972 / 713138001 — Assistance with mobility in bed (SNOMED)*.
- *1242274 / 12101000202101 — Assistance with getting out of bed (SNOMED)*.
- *46272440 / 710804006 — Assistance with walking using device (SNOMED)* and *46271857 / 710120003 — Promotion of walking using mobility aid (SNOMED)*.
- *4040262 / 229595009 — Walking with patient - mobilization (SNOMED)*, *4204159 / 308931006 — Walking practice (SNOMED)*, *4158838 / 370871008 — Ambulation therapy (SNOMED)*.

## Mapping Notes

- Map records of the patient being mobilized / ambulated (in bed, out of bed, walking, with or without assistance/aids) to the matching concept here; use *46272565 / 710950005 — Progressive mobility* as the general early-mobility concept when not otherwise specified.
- **Patient mobilization, not joint/organ mobilization**: manual-therapy joint mobilizations (e.g. 'accessory mobilization of the shoulder') and surgical organ mobilizations (e.g. 'mobilization of intestine') are *not* part of this set — they were removed.
- **Counterpart of 'Order not to mobilize'**: this set is the act of mobilizing; the order not to mobilize (bed rest directive) is a separate concept set.

fr.name: Mobilisation
fr.shortDescription: Mobilisation du patient — mise en mouvement (au lit, hors du lit, debout, marche, avec ou sans aide) ; l’acte, non la mobilisation articulaire/chirurgicale.

createdByDetails.affiliation: Univ Rennes, CHU Rennes, INSERM, LTSI-UMR 1099, Rennes, France
createdByDetails.profession: Medical Informatics and Intensive Care Physician

versions.author: Boris Delange
versions.message: Rescope to patient mobilization (early-mobility/ambulation acts): replaced the ~140-concept SNOMED "mobilization" tree (which mixed manual-therapy joint mobilizations and surgical organ mobilizations) with a curated list of patient-mobilization procedures (Progressive mobility, Promotion of physical mobility, Assistance with mobility/in bed/getting out of bed/walking, Walking with patient, Walking practice, Ambulation therapy). Counterpart of set 295. Added description.
